description: Anchor signal set for fertility-adjacent medical devices targeting independent OBGYN and general fertility practices that offer IUI as a primary treatment pathway. Practices with active IVF programs or IVF-specialist staff are friction accounts — they protect IVF revenue and are unlikely to prioritize IUI optimization.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the practice website explicitly list intrauterine insemination (IUI) as a service they perform?
signals.notes: Core procedure match. Confirmed absent is near-disqualifying.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website indicate that ultrasound is performed in-office (e.g., transvaginal ultrasound, follicle monitoring, sonohysterography)?
signals.notes: Clinical workflow prerequisite. Confirmed absent is significant friction.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the practice list a Reproductive Endocrinologist and Infertility (REI) specialist among its physicians?
signals.notes: Friction signal for IUI-optimization products. REIs are IVF-trained and IVF-revenue motivated. Their presence signals IVF-first prioritization over IUI workflow improvement.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is infertility treatment listed as a named service, specialty, or department (not merely mentioned in passing)?
Does the website mention male-factor infertility, semen analysis, or male reproductive services?
Is the practice independently owned — not part of a hospital system, academic medical center, or large health network?
signals.notes: Independent practices have direct budget authority. Hospital affiliation triggers procurement committees and extends deal cycles.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website indicate affiliation with a hospital, academic medical center, or large health system?
signals.notes: Friction signal. Confirmed affiliation caps at Watchlist — hospital procurement kills deal velocity.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website list in vitro fertilization (IVF) as an on-site service performed at this location?
signals.notes: Friction signal for IUI-optimization products. An active IVF program means the practice protects IVF revenue per cycle. These practices route patients toward IVF, not toward improving IUI outcomes. Caps at Watchlist.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website use language indicating cash pay, self-pay pricing, affordability, or cost-conscious fertility options?
signals.notes: Patient mix signal — receptive to cost-saving clinical tools.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website list saline infusion sonography (SIS/SHG) or sonohysterography as a service?
signals.notes: Adjacent in-office procedure — confirms the practice runs in-office diagnostics.

Applied downstream from a static state-law lookup. NOT scraped from the practice website. Practices in states with infertility insurance mandates face formulary and reimbursement scrutiny that adds procurement friction. Non-mandate states have fewer barriers to a direct purchase decision.
